title: osbeckia muralis
intro: Osbeckia muralis, also known as red maire or common osbeckia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae. Traditionally, the plant is used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Osbeckia muralis include treating fever, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as a remedy for snake bites. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture for herbal use, although its effectiveness may vary depending on the preparation method.
